title: Hesperolinon micranthum
intro: Hesperolinon micranthum, also known as dwarf flax lily or little yellow star, is a small, annual or perennial plant that belongs to the family Liliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Hesperolinon micranthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hesperolinon micranthum is commonly prepared as a tincture or tea, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Hesperolinon micranthum is a species of flowering plant in the family Liliaceae. It is a rare and endemic species found in the southwestern United States. Hesperolinon micranthum is commonly known as small-flowered tower lily or dwarf tower lily. The plant is typically found growing in grasslands and open areas of the southwestern deserts. As a member of the family Liliaceae, Hesperolinon micranthum is closely related to other lily species.
